description:
You are a QA engineer who repeatedly spins-up disposable, timestamped test environments.  
Automate this workflow with a short shell script and then demonstrate that it works.

Target script  
• Path: /home/user/scripts/setup_test_env.sh  
• Length expectation: roughly 5-10 shell commands (logic and comments not counted toward the limit).  
• Interpreter line: #!/usr/bin/env bash  

Functional requirements  
1. The script must accept exactly one positional argument: ENV_NAME (e.g. “staging”, “qa”).  
2. It must build a new environment directory whose name is the value of ENV_NAME followed by an underscore and the current timestamp in the form YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS; for example /home/user/test_envs/qa_20241103_142501.  
   • Parent directory /home/user/test_envs is guaranteed to exist; create it if it does not.  
3. Inside the new environment directory create three sub-directories:  
   • logs  
   • data  
   • config  
4. Inside the config sub-directory place a file named env.conf with exactly three lines (key=value pairs):  
      DB_HOST=localhost  
      DB_PORT=5432  
      API_KEY=<RANDOM_16_HEX_CHARS>  
   • <RANDOM_16_HEX_CHARS> must be a 16-character lowercase hexadecimal string (0-9, a-f) generated at runtime.  
   • No extra whitespace, blank lines or trailing spaces.  
5. In the root of the environment directory create README.md whose first line is a level-1 Markdown heading of the form  
      # <ENV_NAME> Test Environment created on <ISO8601_TIMESTAMP>  
   • ISO8601 timestamp must include date, time, and timezone offset, for example 2024-11-03T14:25:01+00:00.  
6. Append a single summary line to the central log file /home/user/test_envs/env_creation.log using this exact comma-separated format (no spaces):  
      <ISO8601_TIMESTAMP>,<ENV_NAME>,<FULL_PATH_TO_CREATED_ENV_DIRECTORY>  
   • Each invocation of the script must append (not overwrite).  
   • The line must end with a newline character.  
7. The script must exit with status 0 when successful.  
8. Make the script executable by the current user.

Demonstration steps you must perform after writing the script  
A. Execute the script once with the argument qa.  
B. Show the final five lines of /home/user/test_envs/env_creation.log to prove your run was logged.  
C. Display (cat) the contents of the generated env.conf file to prove the key/value layout and the 16-character API_KEY.  

Formatting rules the automated grader will check  
1. The newest line in /home/user/test_envs/env_creation.log must match the regular expression  
      ^[0-9T:+-]{20,25},qa,/home/user/test_envs/qa_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}$  
2. env.conf must consist of exactly three newline-separated lines whose third line’s value matches  
      ^[0-9a-f]{16}$  
3. README.md’s first line must begin with “# qa Test Environment created on ” and contain a valid ISO8601 timestamp.

Finish when you have:  
• The executable script at the required path.  
• A successful run with ENV_NAME “qa”.  
• The requested log and file outputs shown in the terminal.
truth:
The automated tests will perform these checks:

1. Script presence and permissions  
   • File /home/user/scripts/setup_test_env.sh exists and is executable (mode 755 or 750).

2. Single recent environment directory  
   • Using the env_creation.log last line containing “,qa,” the grader will extract FULL_PATH; that directory must exist and contain sub-directories logs, data, config plus README.md and config/env.conf.

3. Log correctness  
   • The last line of /home/user/test_envs/env_creation.log must match  
        ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(?:[+-][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2})?,qa,/home/user/test_envs/qa_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\n$  

4. env.conf format  
   • Exactly three lines:  
        Line1 == “DB_HOST=localhost”  
        Line2 == “DB_PORT=5432”  
        Line3 matches ^API_KEY=[0-9a-f]{16}$  

5. README.md heading  
   • First line equals /^# qa Test Environment created on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(?:[+-][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2})?$/  

No other content inside env.conf; additional lines are disallowed.

Initial system assumptions  
• Directory /home/user/scripts exists and is writable.  
• Directory /home/user/test_envs may or may not exist; tests create it if necessary before evaluation.
